rulings.Case Title: PAUL A WAERDT PHD VS. THE HARCOURT GROUP, LLC ET AL
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for Friday May 6, 2022, line 1. DEFENDANT SOJOURN PROPERTIES, INC., GALLO DBIZ, HARCOURT GROUP, LLC's Motion For Entry Of Judgment. Defendants' Motion for Entry of Judgment is GRANTED. No opposition filed. Parties may appear in-person, telephonically or via Zoom (Video - Webinar ID: 836 2280 0051; Password: 613303; or Phone Dial in: (415) 762-9988; Webinar ID: 836 2280 0051; Password: 613303). Parties who intend to appear at the hearing must give notice to opposing parties and the court promptly, but no later than 4:00 p.m. the court day before the hearing unless the tentative ruling has specified that a hearing is required. Notice of contesting a tentative ruling shall be provided by sending an email to the court to Department501ContestTR@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. A party may not argue at the hearing if the opposing party is not so notified and the opposing party does not appear.= (501/CFH)

rulings.Case Title: JONES MEMORIAL HOMES, INC. VS. MARK ANTHONY SMITH
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To Vacate January 20, 2022 Order Sustaining Defendant Mark Smith'S Demurrer To Plaintiff'S First Amended Complaint
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for Friday May 6, 2022, line 4. PLAINTIFF JONES MEMORIAL HOMES, INC.'s Motion To Vacate January 20, 2022 Order Sustaining Defendant Mark Smith'S Demurrer To Plaintiff'S First Amended Complaint. Plaintiff's Motion to Vacate January 20, 2022 Order Sustaining Defendant's Demurrer to Plaintiff's First Amended Complaint is GRANTED. No later than May 6, 2022 by 5:00 p.m. Plaintiff to properly file and personally/overnight mail serve its opposition to the demurrer. Demurrer hearing shall be set by the Court for May 20, 2022. By no later that May 9, 2022 Plaintiff to provide courtesy copies of the demurrer and opposition to department 501 with a cover letter reflecting new hearing date. Defendant shall have until May 17, 2022 to file a reply. Parties may appear in-person, telephonically or via Zoom (Video - Webinar ID: 836 2280 0051; Password: 613303; or Phone Dial in: (415) 762-9988; Webinar ID: 836 2280 0051; Password: 613303). Parties who intend to appear at the hearing must give notice to opposing parties and the court promptly, but no later than 4:00 p.m. the court day before the hearing unless the tentative ruling has specified that a hearing is required. Notice of contesting a tentative ruling shall be provided by sending an email to the court to Department501ContestTR@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. A party may not argue at the hearing if the opposing party is not so notified and the opposing party does not appear.= (501/CFH)

rulings.Case Title: STORY RENTALS, INC. VS. BIANCA KRIEG ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion For Order To Release Funds Held By Court
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for Friday May 6, 2022, line 5. PLAINTIFF STORY RENTALS, INC.'s Motion For Order To Release Funds Held By Court. Plaintiff's Motion for Order to Release Funds held by Court is OFF CALENDAR. No proof of service for this motion on file. Proof of Service attached to the Notice of Motion is for "Notice of Entry of Judgment." A valid Proof of service must be on file at least 5 days before the hearing. CRC 3.1300(c). Parties may appear in-person, telephonically or via Zoom (Video - Webinar ID: 836 2280 0051; Password: 613303; or Phone Dial in: (415) 762-9988; Webinar ID: 836 2280 0051; Password: 613303). Parties who intend to appear at the hearing must give notice to opposing parties and the court promptly, but no later than 4:00 p.m. the court day before the hearing unless the tentative ruling has specified that a hearing is required. Notice of contesting a tentative ruling shall be provided by sending an email to the court to Department501ContestTR@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. A party may not argue at the hearing if the opposing party is not so notified and the opposing party does not appear.= (501/CFH)
